---
title: "Configuring The User Group Mappings"
---

---
title: "Configuring the User Group Mappings"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-for-Salesforce/rvp1756295738548"
section: "Hyland for Salesforce"
---

This section describes how to assign permissions for members of existing User Groups.

You must configure User Roles for each User Group to ensure members of User Groups have the necessary permissions. These permissions include the ability to view and capture supported files and configure settings related to these tasks from the Hyland for Salesforce application.
To configure User Group Mappings:

1.  Open a web browser and log on to the Salesforce environment with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  From the top-right of the Home page, click the App Launcher button.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,) ![App Launcher button]()   The App Launcher drop-down menu is displayed.
3.  In the App Launcher search box, enter Application Configuration. The search results are displayed.
4.  Click Application Configurations from the search results. The Application Configurations login page is displayed.
5.  Log on to the Hyland for Salesforce application if you aren't authenticated already. The Application Configuration page is displayed.
6.  In the User Group Name field, enter one of the following:
-
Salesforce Content User

-
Salesforce Solution Administrator

Note: Verify that Roles and their associated User Groups are properly configured using the Hyland Content Innovation Cloud Administration Portal application. If they are not configured yet, contact your solution provider and complete the instructions under Configuring User Groups and Roles.
7.  From the User Role Name drop-down list, select one of the following options:
| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Content User | Assigning this Role to the selected User Group allows members to view, capture, and add metadata to supported files using Hyland for Salesforce. |
| Solution Admin | Assigning this Role to the selected User Group grants members access to the Solution Configurations and Application Configurations sections within Hyland for Salesforce, allowing them to manage configuration settings. |

8.  Review the information and then from the top-right, click Save.
